title: Economy vs Markets
text: A recurring question: how tightly is the stock market coupled to economic growth? This page puts the two side-by-side, looks at correlations, and lets you drill into S&P 500 sector performance by year.

title: Economy vs Markets
section: Annual GDP growth vs S&P 500 return
text:
Annual GDP growth vs S&P 500 return
Two very different pictures in the same window — equity returns are far more volatile than GDP.

title: Economy vs Markets
text:
How correlated are they, really?
Each point is a year. Hover to see the year label. The 2020 COVID year and the 2022 inflation shock pull the relationship around quite a bit.

title: Economy vs Markets
section: S&P 500 sector returns — drill by decade → year → sector
text:
S&P 500 sector returns — drill by decade → year → sector
Treemap with native drill-down. Click a decade tile to zoom into its years; click a year tile to see its sector-by-sector return breakdown. Sector tile size is scaled by absolute return; color shows sign (green = positive, red = negative).

title: Economy vs Markets
text:
Cross-indicator heatmap
Correlations across indicators, 2001–2025. Click a cell for the exact value.

title: US Economy 2026
text: This site is an interactive drill-down analysis of the US economy in 2026 placed against the past 25 years (2001–2025). Use the top navigation to move between the four panels, or click the links below.

title: US Economy 2026
text:
Where to go next

Economic Growth → — GDP growth, quarterly detail, and GDP components (consumption / investment / government / net exports).
Indicators → — Unemployment, inflation, rates, VIX — filterable table with inline sparklines; click a row for the full time series.
Economy vs Markets → — Do markets actually track economic growth? Correlation scatter, dual-axis view, and sector treemap.

title: About this prototype
text: A static, open-source prototype that analyzes the US economy in 2026 against the prior 25 years and compares economic fundamentals to market performance. Every chart is an interactive JavaScript widget rendered into a self-contained HTML page — no server, no database, no paid libraries.

title: About this prototype
text: A static, open-source prototype that analyzes the US economy in 2026 against the prior 25 years and compares economic fundamentals to market performance. Every chart is an interactive JavaScript widget rendered into a self-contained HTML page — no server, no database, no paid libraries.

title: About this prototype
text:
Data
This prototype is a calibrated synthetic dataset, not live data.

Annual values for 2001–2024 are hand-entered from public sources: BEA (GDP, GDP components), BLS (unemployment, CPI), Federal Reserve (Fed Funds, 10Y Treasury), NBER (recession dating), and public index aggregates (S&P 500, Dow, NASDAQ, VIX). Exact vintage-adjusted official values may differ slightly.
Values for 2025 and 2026 are illustrative prototype values. They are not forecasts and should not be treated as such. Any year ≥ 2025 is flagged with prototype = 1 in the CSV and marked with an asterisk in the UI.
Quarterly and monthly series are decomposed from the annual series with seeded noise so that quarterly/monthly averages match the annual figure.


Swapping in live data
R/generate_data.R is the only place data is produced. To use live FRED data:

install.packages("fredr") and set a FRED API key via fredr_set_key().
Replace the annual tibble::tribble(...) block with:
fetch <- function(id) fredr::fredr(series_id = id, frequency = "a")
annual <- reduce(list(
  fetch("GDPC1")  |> transmute(year = year(date), gdp_growth  = value),
  fetch("UNRATE") |> transmute(year = year(date), unemployment = value),
  fetch("CPIAUCSL") |> transmute(year = year(date), cpi = value),
  fetch("FEDFUNDS") |> transmute(year = year(date), fed_funds = value),
  fetch("DGS10")  |> transmute(year = year(date), ten_year = value),
  fetch("SP500")  |> transmute(year = year(date), sp500_ret = value)
), full_join, by = "year")
Re-run Rscript R/generate_data.R, then quarto render.

title: About this prototype
text:
Regenerating the site
Rscript R/generate_data.R   # rebuild CSVs (only needed if data changes)
quarto render               # rebuild HTML into _site/

title: About this prototype
text:
Publishing
A GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/publish.yml runs on every push to main, renders the site, and pushes _site/ to the gh-pages branch. Enable Pages in repo settings to serve from gh-pages.

title: About this prototype
text:
Known limitations

Synthetic 2025–2026 values — see above.
Quarterly/monthly series are interpolated, not actual vintage data. High-frequency movements within a year are illustrative only.
No intraday data — daily and intraday series would require a larger dataset than is useful for a static prototype.
No nominal/real adjustment is done on market returns; they are reported as nominal total returns.

title: Economic Growth
text: Use the tabs below to drill from the annual headline into quarterly detail and into the GDP expenditure components. The sunburst at the bottom lets you drill into any single year’s composition interactively (click a wedge to zoom in; click the center to zoom out).

title: Economic Indicators
text:
Rate environment in context
Fed Funds vs 10Y Treasury. When the curve inverts (Fed Funds > 10Y), a recession has historically followed within ~18 months.
